mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient intercepts the switch CMP on the 4-byte DeviceClass FOURCC, preserving inputs progressively Hamming-closer to 'prtr', which naive and cmplog cannot sense without the distance bucket.
one_line: VP gradient climbs 4-byte DeviceClass FOURCC toward 'prtr'

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward incremental progress on every comparison gating the multi-stage XML prologue and conditional-section keyword path, letting VP variants climb gates that naive and cmplog stall on.
one_line: VP gradient climbs multi-stage XML parser to conditional-section keyword error

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP records distance buckets on RAW!=';' and the digit-range compares, preserving inputs that bring scanned bytes closer to ';' or into '0'-'9', letting VP ratchet toward a well-formed '&#<digits>;' charref termination.
one_line: VP gradient assembles decimal charref digit run terminating at ';'

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward incremental progress on multi-byte token comparisons inside the DTD external-subset loop, ratcheting the corpus toward a structurally valid body that drives the loop to completion rather than halting.
one_line: VP gradient climbs DTD external-subset body comparisons to complete the parse loop

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward each byte getting closer to the 'xmlns'/'xml:' cascade comparisons, letting VP climb the multi-byte namespace-declaration gate that naive and cmplog must brute-force.
one_line: VP gradient climbs xmlns/xml prefix comparison cascade into xmlNewNs

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient gives partial-match coverage credit on the byte-wise xmlns comparison cascade, letting VP incrementally assemble the literal 'xmlns' attribute name and reach the NULL-prefix call site that naive/cmplog cannot get steady feedback toward.
one_line: VP gradient assembles 'xmlns' attribute name to pass NULL prefix into xmlNewNs

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets preserve inputs that move parser comparison operands closer to matching, gradient-climbing the string/structure needed to build and free a prefix-bearing xmlNs node that naive and cmplog cannot incrementally discover.
one_line: VP gradient climbs parse path to construct prefixed namespace node for xmlFreeNs

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance feedback preserves inputs approaching the well-formed xmlns= default-namespace declaration, building a nsDef with prefix==NULL and href!=NULL that naive/cmplog edge feedback cannot reward.
one_line: VP gradient builds default-namespace nsDef with prefix==NULL for xmlSearchNs

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance buckets on xmlIsDocNameChar's character-class comparisons reward inputs as a candidate byte approaches the boundary of the rejected NameChar set, letting VP evolve a leading character that fails the test.
one_line: VP gradient walks leading attribute byte to outside accepted NameChar set

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient rewards partial progress on the comparison sequence the parser must satisfy to construct a default-namespace node (cur->prefix==NULL), ratcheting toward the NULL-prefix structure that naive and cmplog cannot incrementally discover.
one_line: VP gradient constructs default-namespace node with NULL prefix for serializer

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback rewards progressive prefix matches on SQL keyword/operator token comparisons, letting value_profile incrementally assemble SELECT-with-shift syntax that naive and cmplog (lacking CMP_MAP) stall on.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient assembles valid SELECT with shift operator to reach OP_ShiftLeft

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient rewards each incremental byte-match toward the dot-separated X.Y.Z token structure; near-matches on tokenizer/parser CMPs are preserved, letting value_profile climb to the rare two-dot qualified-name layout that naive cannot assemble.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient assembles two-dot qualified name so zDb != null

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient on sqlite3StrICmp calls rewards inputs whose table/column token is Hamming-closer to a real schema name, preserving partial-match seeds until cntTab increments, which naive's edge-only feedback cannot gradient toward.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient matches column token to schema name so cntTab increments

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback preserves inputs whose integer/relational CMP operand pairs are closer to matching, letting value_profile incrementally build the operator-laden expression that reaches the codegen arm; naive and cmplog stall on trivial SELECTs.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient builds multi-operator expression to reach expression codegen path

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient writes Hamming/prefix-distance buckets for each lexer/parser integer and string CMP, preserving inputs that partially match keyword tokens; value_profile ratchets toward the full token sequence gating sqlite3TwoPartName that naive and cmplog cannot climb.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient climbs chained parser comparisons to reach sqlite3TwoPartName true branch

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient records Hamming/prefix-distance buckets for lexer/parser string and integer CMPs on the VACUUM keyword path; partial-match inputs are preserved, letting value_profile climb to the valid VACUUM-reduce parse that naive and cmplog (lacking CMP_MAP) cannot ratchet toward.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient assembles VACUUM keyword parse to reach sqlite3Vacuum true branch

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient rewards partial keyword matches (e.g. progressively matching 'vacuum') with new coverage buckets, preserving those seeds and letting value_profile climb toward the exact grammar-token sequence that drives the LALR parser to the yy_reduce rule.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient climbs keyword token matches to reach LALR parser reduce at yy_reduce
